title: 3-Flow Conjecture (Tutte)
context_text: Tutte proved that a planar graph admits a 3-colouring if and only if its dual admits a nowhere-zero 3-flow, so Grötzsch's theorem implies 4-edge-connected planar graphs have nowhere-zero 3-flows. Tutte conjectured planarity is not required. The paper's density results on flow-critical graphs and its extension of the Lovász et al. theorem are motivated as a possible approach toward this conjecture.
paper_contribution: Proves a density bound $|E(G)| \leq 3|V(G)| - 5$ for flow-critical graphs with at most one vertex of degree at least 7 (Theorem 1.6), and extends the Lovász et al. 6-edge-connectivity theorem to allow the tip vertex $z$ to have arbitrary degree when another high-degree vertex exists with no small separating cut.

title: Conjecture 1.4 (Li et al.)
statement_text: For any connected-flow-critical graph $G$ on at least seven vertices, $|E(G)| \leq 3|V(G)| - 8$.
context_text: Li et al. proposed this upper bound on edge density for connected-flow-critical graphs, noting it implies Theorem 1.3 (Lovász et al.) and is achieved by infinitely many examples. The paper states Conjecture 1.4 is still wide open aside from lower-bound progress in [11] and genus-bounded cases in [6], but proves an analogous bound for the more general notion of flow-criticality.
paper_contribution: Proves the analogous density bound $|E(G)| \leq 3|V(G)| - 5$ for (non-connected) flow-critical graphs $G$ with at most one vertex of degree at least 7 (Theorem 1.6), a strictly more general graph class than connected-flow-critical graphs.

title: Conjecture 1.5 (Li et al.)
statement_text: For any connected-flow-critical graph $G$ on at least seven vertices with $n_3$ vertices of degree 3, we have $|E(G)| < \frac{5|V(G)|}{2} + n_3$.
context_text: Li et al. proposed this stronger density bound incorporating the count $n_3$ of degree-3 vertices, motivated by the observation that the extremal examples for Conjecture 1.4 all have many degree-3 vertices. If true, this stronger bound would imply the 3-flow conjecture. The paper notes this conjecture as background motivation alongside Conjecture 1.4.
paper_contribution: Not directly proved; the paper's Theorem 1.6 establishes a related but weaker density bound for (non-connected) flow-critical graphs restricted to those with at most one vertex of degree at least 7.
